data.settleTest: A Europe- or CE-7-scoped segment capacity split from CBRE, Synergy, or JLL showing hyperscale-owned share of CE-7 megawatts today. This is a purchasable/publishable dataset describing today's geographic distribution of installed capacity, not a future market outcome.

proposedCorrection.rationale: CBRE's evidence that ~60% of European hyperscale self-build capacity sits in Ireland, Netherlands, Sweden, and Belgium (outside CE-7) means the global 48%×0.91 carve-out overstates CE-7 hyperscale share by roughly a third; rescaling to CE-7 geography lands near 33%.

researchQueries.query: CBRE or JLL Central & Eastern Europe (Warsaw, Prague, Budapest, Bucharest, Vienna) data center market report giving hyperscale self-build vs colocation vs enterprise capacity share of CE-7 megawatts
Synergy Research or DatacenterDynamics regional split showing hyperscale-owned capacity at 40% or more of Central Europe / CEE installed megawatts
